methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Hippocratic AI is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Munjal Shah that has raised $120M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (Palantir), with primary focus on 5 same-stage, same-sector peers spanning valuations from $500M to $32.0B.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $120M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $228M. Peer-median analysis examines 5 direct comparables—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—producing a median implied valuation of $5.0B. The wide range ($500M to $32.0B) reflects material dispersion within the frontier AI subsector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $194M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The stage-progression approach anchors to company-specific deployment capital but may undervalue frontier AI's outsized risk-return profile. The peer-median approach captures market-clearing valuations but is sensitive to outliers like Safe Superintelligence. Key uncertainties include the durability of frontier AI valuations, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether Hippocratic AI's medical focus commands sector-specific premiums or discounts.
